updated: July 22, 2026, afternoon check. Two things closed today. DTE's Jon Johnson picked Wednesday July 29 at noon, so his session moves to Week 4 and Week 3 is fully locked. He asked for a calendar invite plus a contact name and phone number for the day of, so send that. And Danielle Boone at Connect Detroit confirmed the Young Professionals Conference registration is received, 3 adults and 16 participants, and no one can be added after the fact. Next up: staff the pay card pickup Thursday July 23 at 613 Abbott Street, pay the CPR invoice by July 27, and reply to the three virtual requests.
headline: Pay card pickup is set: Thursday July 23, 9:30 to 5 at 613 Abbott Street, by appointment. Schedule the Calendly slot, send a rep, bring documentation of time worked. Cards go to the agency, not the youth. The CPR invoice came in at $442 and Kamilia approved it, so pay Rhythm of Life by July 27. DTE took Wednesday July 29 at noon, so Week 3 is locked and Week 4 now has DTE, SEL and CPR on it. Send Jon the invite and a day-of contact.

week1.note: Canceled. Orientation moves to Wednesday. Everyone still gets paid for this day. Families were notified the night of July 6.

week1.note: Done. Virtual and self-paced. Youth submitted their Capstone Kickoff on their own time. This closed out Week 1.

doNow.title: Payroll 2 Transcript submitted
doNow.desc: Sent July 20 at 2:23 PM to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org, cc Gwendolyn and Je'Tina, in before the 5 PM deadline. GDYT replied Received on July 21, so the transcript is confirmed in.

doNow.title: Schedule and staff the Payroll 2 pay card pickup, Thursday 7/23
doNow.desc: Dawn at Connect Detroit set it July 20. Pickup is Thursday July 23, 9:30 to 5, at Connect Detroit, 613 Abbott Street, Detroit 48226, Amphitheatre on the lower level, by appointment only. Schedule your slot on the Calendly link, email gdyt@connectdetroit.org when you are 10 minutes out with NAACP in the message, and bring documentation of time worked for each youth. An agency rep picks up all 15 cards, do not send the young people. Once you have them, check them against the list, report any missing to Dawn, and have each youth scan the QR code or use the Jotform link to acknowledge receipt. Cards cannot be mailed to youth. Jordan-Kallel's father asked to pick up his son's card, so tell families the Branch collects the cards and hands them out.

doNow.title: YPC registration confirmed
doNow.desc: Danielle Boone at Connect Detroit confirmed July 22 that the registration came through. Je'Tina submitted the form for 3 adults and 16 participants. Danielle was clear that they go by the form only, they did not compare it against the attendee list Je'Tina emailed, and they cannot add anyone who was not on the submission. So the head count is fixed at what went in. The workshop half was already closed out, Jermaine passed on presenting because he is out of the country August 4 to 11 and Shuna accepted that July 17.

doNow.title: Cover the banking side of Week 2 (Huntington out)
doNow.desc: Courtney Elledge at Huntington wrote back and cannot do Banking 101 this year. She offered to send financial education and banking materials for the youth to use during the week. So now both Banking 101 and the credit session still need a plan. Take her materials, and either find a fast replacement or have Jermaine run Banking 101. Week 2 is already underway, so this needs a plan now.

doNow.title: Pay the CPR invoice, due July 27
doNow.desc: The price question is answered. Natosha Gurley at Rhythm of Life sent invoice #M4ITOVW8-0001 on July 20. It covers 16 youth at $12 each ($192) and 5 staff or youth council at the AHA Friends and Family rate of $50 each ($250), for $442 total, due July 27. Kamilia approved it July 21. Pay it before the 27th.

doNow.title: Confirm all 15 are on the final GDYT approved list
doNow.desc: Nicole Hill's July 14 update says the Youth Enrollment Tracker now holds the final Approved for Placement list, and only youth on it can work or be paid. Youth who worked before being approved cannot be paid for that time, and GDYT will not cover unapproved youth. Check the YET and confirm all 15 of ours show Approved for Placement. Also get the worksite ready for GDYT monitor visits over the next few weeks: original work permits on hand and a first aid kit.

doNow.title: Send Connect Detroit the payroll backup docs
doNow.desc: Now that the transcript is in, they need your proof: signed daily sign-in sheets and benchmark tracking. Remaining pay is held until Connect Detroit has it.

doNow.title: Get the 15 youth into the GDYT Empower App
doNow.desc: Kelsey at Connect Detroit sent the app sign-up reminder with a youth instructions deck. The app's modules also satisfy the GDYT financial literacy requirement, and it's separate from payroll. The GDYT App team now sends an automatic weekly module progress report, the first arrived July 20 at 6 AM listing which youth have signed up and their module counts. Anyone not on the report has not signed up yet. Sign up group code is naacp-1024 at empowerme.app/gdyt, so use the report to chase whoever is still missing.

doNow.title: Lock the rest of Week 4
doNow.desc: CPR is locked for Thursday July 30 (approved) and DTE is locked for Wednesday July 29 at noon. Still need Keosha's health day and Zipporah's Financial Wellness date. Noon on July 29 is now DTE's, so Keosha goes to July 30 or to a different hour on the 29th. Ask her which she wants.

doNow.title: Send Jon Johnson the invite and a day-of contact
doNow.desc: DTE is locked. Jon wrote back July 22 and took Wednesday July 29 at 12:00, in person at the Branch. He asked for two things: a calendar invite for the day and time, and a contact name with a phone number in case he needs help getting in. Send both. His session lands in Week 4, not Week 3, so Week 3 is now fully set.

doNow.title: Answer the three Week 3 virtual requests
doNow.desc: Now three young people. A brother and sister both emailed July 12 asking to work virtually July 20 to 29, and they are fully out July 21 to 24. Jordan-Kallel's father wrote July 20 that his son is out of town this week at the USA Cycling Nationals and asked for the week's assignments to complete while away. That window covers the in-person heart of Week 3 (Dream Car Reveal Tue 7/21, Entrepreneur Panel Thu 7/23). Reply to all three with a virtual plan so they stay on payroll and don't fall behind on the Capstone.

doNow.title: Confirm the two bounced families are set for the rest
doNow.desc: Two addresses bounced on the July 6 all-family email. Week 1 is done. Confirm both families by text or call that they have the schedule for the remaining weeks. One of the two also has a working gmail on the same thread.

waiting.name: Jessica Archer (Wayne State)
waiting.note: Answered July 15 at 5:14. You asked the group that morning and sent her the whole list: campus life, the law school and the professor named Ryan who got the biggest reaction, the BSMD eight year medical track, engineering and the new building, business and marketing, scholarships and internships, both asked unprompted, the Transfer Lounge, dorms, the Student Center, D9 and the black student orgs, the gym and the basketball courts, the game room, the Wright Museum, food, parking. Plus your two: what it takes to get in and what it costs, and a VP or dean on how to actually use a campus and its resources. You offered roster, forms and waivers same day. Now she builds the July 28 schedule and confirms lunch.

waiting.name: Zipporah Bolar
waiting.note: Checking her schedule for the Financial Wellness session (Week 2 or 4).

waiting.name: GDYT payroll + pay cards
waiting.note: Payroll 2 pivoted again July 17. myOneFlow will not be ready, so no one touches the system. Each partner fills out one customized Payroll Transcript, the Excel form Connect Detroit sends, and emails it to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org by 5:00 PM Monday July 20. No other format accepted, one per agency. Tab A is total benchmarks completed per youth for July 6 to 10 and July 13 to 17, Tab B is the authorization signature. The file is sorted, the first attachment was named for Mack Alive but Nicole corrected it and the right form is named NAACP Hybrid Payroll #2 Transcript. Backup documentation, your own signed sign in and benchmark sheets, is still required and remaining pay is held until Connect Detroit has it. Only youth on the final Approved for Placement list in the Youth Enrollment Tracker are paid, so confirm all 15 are on it (see Do Now), and GDYT monitors will visit to check safety, supervision, original work permits and a first aid kit. Our group is Payroll 2, first pay July 31. Dawn confirmed the pay card pickup July 20: Thursday July 23, 9:30 to 5, at Connect Detroit, 613 Abbott Street, by Calendly appointment, agency rep only. Bring documentation of time worked, do not send youth, and youth acknowledge receipt by QR code. Cards cannot be mailed to youth (see Do Now). GDYT replied Received on July 21, so the transcript is confirmed in.

waiting.note: Kelsey (Connect Detroit) sent app sign-up instructions Jul 6, with a youth how-to deck attached. Youth register in the Empower App; its modules cover the GDYT financial literacy requirement (separate from payroll). The GDYT App team now sends an automatic weekly module progress report, the first arrived July 20 at 6 AM with per youth module counts, and anyone missing from it has not signed up yet. Sign up group code naacp-1024 at empowerme.app/gdyt.
